subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Jogger Velocity Table — Rate Approximation, Right Riemann Sum, Acceleration & Avg Velocity
stimulus:
Johanna jogs along a straight path. For $0 \le t \le 40$, Johanna's velocity is given by a differentiable function $v$. Selected values of $v(t)$, where $t$ is measured in minutes and $v(t)$ is measured in meters per minute, are given in the table.

| $t$ (minutes) | 0 | 12 | 20 | 24 | 40 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| $v(t)$ (meters per minute) | 0 | 200 | 240 | $-220$ | 150 |

parts.prompt: Use the data in the table to estimate the value of $v'(16)$.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct estimate using difference quotient: $v'(16) \approx \frac{v(20) - v(12)}{20 - 12} = \frac{240 - 200}{8} = 5$ meters per minute per minute
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $v'(16) \approx \frac{240-200}{20-12} = 5$ meters per minute$^2$.

parts.prompt: Using correct units, explain the meaning of the definite integral $\int_0^{40} v(t)\, dt$ in the context of the problem. Approximate the value of $\int_0^{40} v(t)\, dt$ using a right Riemann sum with the four subintervals indicated in the table.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct explanation with units: $\int_0^{40} v(t)\, dt$ is the total distance Johanna jogs, in meters, over the time interval $0 \le t \le 40$ minutes (or: net displacement in meters)
1 pt: Correct right Riemann sum: $12 \cdot v(12) + 8 \cdot v(20) + 4 \cdot v(24) + 16 \cdot v(40)$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Right sum $= 12(200) + 8(240) + 4(-220) + 16(150) = 5840$ meters. (Scoring key shows $4 \cdot 220$ without negative: $= 7600$; check if table value at $t=24$ is $-220$ or $220$... Scoring key uses $4 \cdot 220$, yielding $7600$. Table note: $v(24) = -220$... but scoring key: $= 12(200)+8(240)+4(220)+16(150) = 7600$. Accept answer based on signed value if student reads $-220$.)

parts.prompt: Bob is riding his bicycle along the same path. For $0 \le t \le 10$, Bob's velocity is modeled by $B(t) = t^3 - 6t^2 + 300$, where $t$ is measured in minutes and $B(t)$ is measured in meters per minute. Find Bob's acceleration at time $t = 5$.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct answer: $B'(5) = 3(25) - 12(5) = 75 - 60 = 15$ meters per minute per minute
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $B'(t) = 3t^2 - 12t$; $B'(5) = 75 - 60 = 15$ meters per minute$^2$.

parts.prompt: Based on the model $B$ from part (c), find Bob's average velocity during the interval $0 \le t \le 10$.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Sets up average velocity integral: $\frac{1}{10}\int_0^{10} B(t)\, dt = \frac{1}{10}\int_0^{10}(t^3 - 6t^2 + 300)\, dt$
1 pt: Correct answer: $\frac{1}{10}\left(\frac{10000}{4} - 2000 + 3000\right) = \frac{1}{10}(3500) = 350$ meters per minute
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Average velocity $= \frac{1}{10}\left[\frac{t^4}{4} - 2t^3 + 300t\right]_0^{10} = \frac{1}{10}(2500 - 2000 + 3000) = 350$ meters per minute.
